comments.body:
DERISK (E1/E2/E3, real AWS CLI as root of acct 896586841071, us-east-1):
- Default VPC exists: vpc-01fb22d3dd50a364c (172.31.0.0/16).
- AL2023 arm64 AMI resolvable via SSM public param /aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-arm64 (currently ami-06c84fbfd615657d3) — use the SSM param, not a hardcoded id.
- t4g.large offered in all us-east-1 AZs (a/b/c/d/e/f).
- Route53: PUBLIC hosted zone solutions.navateam.com EXISTS in this account (Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ) — create rebar.solutions.navateam.com as an A record (-> EIP) directly in this zone. (Parent is solutions.navateam.com, not navateam.com.)
- SSM SecureString put -> get --with-decryption -> delete round-trip validated (the secrets-at-boot pattern); no lingering param.
- run-instances DRY-RUN with the exact shape (t4g.large, ami-06c84fbfd615657d3, gp3 encrypted root, IMDSv2 HttpTokens=required, default subnet) returned 'Request would have succeeded' — call + IAM perms validated without launching.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S1 implemented + applied LIVE (account 896586841071, us-east-1) — 2026-06-30

Code authored at commit 30c92d4f4 on branch worktree-epic-d251; applied live.

**Live resources (terraform-managed, remote state in s3://rebar-tfstate-896586841071):**
- EC2 instance `i-00880b2c7f13527c5` — t4g.large, arm64, **State=running**, **IMDSv2 HttpTokens=required**.
- EBS data volume `vol-06fa2e77a9dd97527` (gp3, 50 GB, `prevent_destroy`); root gp3 30 GB.
- Elastic IP `52.3.128.230` (`prevent_destroy`); SG `sg-01c907b55cf3c2408` ingress = 443 + 29418 only (no 22).
- Route53 A `rebar.solutions.navateam.com` -> 52.3.128.230 (zone Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ); `dig` resolves.
- DLM policy `policy-0b9639e8c34abcbe9` State=**ENABLED**, retain_rule count=7, DLM service role `rebar-dlm-lifecycle-role` (trust dlm.amazonaws.com).
- Instance role `rebar-gerrit-instance-role`: SSM core + SSM read scoped to `parameter/rebar/prod/*` (no wildcard) + kms:Decrypt via `kms:ViaService=ssm.us-east-1.amazonaws.com` + CloudWatch basic.
- 7 SSM SecureString params under `/rebar/prod/*` created (placeholder CHANGEME) and populated with real values.

**In-box verification (via SSM Session Manager send-command):**
- cloud-init status = **done**.
- `/var/gerrit` mounted from **/dev/nvme1n1** (xfs) — NVMe-by-volume-id resolution worked on Nitro/Graviton; fstab entry by UUID (persists reboot).
- `/etc/rebar/.env` = **0600 root:root**, all 7 secrets present; CHANGEME fail-fast guard exercised (params were populated first, so boot succeeded).

**AC verification:**
- AC1 t4g.large + EIP + gp3 root/data@/var/gerrit + IMDSv2 — verified live.
- AC2 bootstrap one-time local-state; main backend `use_lockfile=true`, no DynamoDB, required_version>=1.10 — applied via S3 backend.
- AC3 prevent_destroy on data vol + EIP + state bucket — set; instance update-in-place (provider v5) confirmed the volume is not recreated.
- AC4 DNS + SG + SSM admin (no 22) — verified.
- AC5 user_data SSM->.env 0600, no plaintext secrets, CHANGEME fail-fast — verified.
- AC6 idempotent blkid-before-mkfs + dynamic NVMe device resolution by volume id — verified (mounted /dev/nvme1n1, no reformat).
- AC7 DLM daily retain=7 + service role ENABLED — verified.
- AC8 IAM SSM/KMS scoped to /rebar/prod/* — verified.
- AC9 ignore_changes=[ami] — set.
- AC10 7 SSM params enumerated by exact name — created.
- AC11 ADR-0012 records four decisions — committed.
- AC12 sdist+wheel exclude infra/ — `python -m build` then grep: 0 infra/ entries in both.

**Code review (subagent):** 1 blocker (missing infra/.gitignore for tfstate/provider binaries) — FIXED (state ignored, lock files tracked, verified `git add -n` stages no state). Comment nit in user_data.sh fixed. Final terraform fmt -check + validate clean; no pending plan diff.

compiled_state.description:
# S1 — Terraform base: EC2 box, networking, DNS subdomain, IAM, SSM secrets, state/backups

## Why
The PoC needs one cost-conscious, reproducible AWS host for Gerrit + the rebar MCP.
Codifying it as IaC is runtime-free and delivers the freeze-and-restore RTO (rebuild
from Terraform) without adding cost — so it serves P3 (reusability) without touching
P2 (cost). Everything else in this epic lands on this substrate.

## What
- One-time bootstrap (`infra/bootstrap/`): an S3 state bucket (versioning + SSE) created
  by a small standalone config applied ONCE manually — this breaks the state-backend
  chicken-and-egg (TF can't store state in a bucket it hasn't created yet). Its bucket
  name feeds the main config's backend block.
- `infra/terraform/` using roll-your-own raw resources (the ec2-instance module adds
  indirection at N=1):
  - `versions.tf`: `required_version = ">= 1.10"` (S3 native `use_lockfile` needs TF
    >=1.10; older TF SILENTLY ignores it and runs with NO locking) + `required_providers`
    (aws ~> 5).
  - `t4g.large` (Graviton, ~8 GB) on AL2023 arm64 — AMI resolved via the SSM public param
    `/aws/service/ami-amazon-linux-latest/al2023-ami-kernel-default-arm64` (NOT a hardcoded
    id); gp3 root + a separate gp3 data volume mounted at `/var/gerrit` with
    `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`; IMDSv2 required.
  - Default VPC; security group inbound 443 (HTTPS) + 29418 (Gerrit SSH); admin access via
    SSM Session Manager (no inbound 22). One Elastic IP, also `prevent_destroy = true`.
  - Route 53 A record `rebar.solutions.navateam.com` -> EIP in the existing public zone
    `Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ` (solutions.navateam.com).
  - IAM instance role: attach `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` (REQUIRED for Session Manager
    — the sole admin path) + SSM Parameter Store read (+ KMS decrypt) + CloudWatch basic.
  - SSM Parameter Store SecureString params (placeholders) for the Gerrit secrets, the
    GitHub replication deploy key, the rebar MCP HMAC signing key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
  - `user_data.sh` (cloud-init): at first boot, fetch the SSM SecureString params via the
    instance role and write them to a `chmod 600` `.env` (NO plaintext secrets in user_data
    itself); IMDSv2 enforced.
  - Terraform S3 backend with `use_lockfile=true` (no DynamoDB); DLM daily EBS snapshots of
    the data volume with a bounded `retain_rule { count = 7 }`.
- Packaging: ensure `infra/` is excluded from ALL distribution channels.

## Scope
In: the bootstrap + TF base + DNS subdomain + IAM + SSM scaffolding + boot script +
state/backup config + the packaging exclusion. Out: Gerrit/MCP containers (S2), Gerrit
config (S3), replication (S5), GitHub lockdown (S6).

## Files
- `infra/bootstrap/main.tf` (one-time state bucket: versioning + SSE)
- `infra/terraform/{versions,main,network,iam,ssm,dns,backup}.tf`
- `infra/terraform/user_data.sh` (SSM-pull -> chmod 600 .env boot script)
- `pyproject.toml` (`[tool.hatch.build.targets.sdist]` include is `["src/rebar","tests","README.md"]`
  so `infra/` is excluded by default — add an explicit exclude + a build-artifact check)
- `docs/adr/0012-iac-foundations.md` (ADR capturing the three novel decisions: the S3 state-bucket bootstrap chicken-and-egg pattern, the no-DynamoDB S3-native locking choice gated on Terraform >=1.10, and the `/rebar/prod/*` SSM parameter naming contract — each with rationale)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `terraform -chdir=infra/terraform validate` and `plan` succeed (TF >=1.10 enforced via
      `required_version`); `apply` stands up a reachable `t4g.large` with EIP, gp3 root + data
      volume mounted at `/var/gerrit`, IMDSv2 enforced.
- [ ] The state-bucket bootstrap (`infra/bootstrap/`) is documented as a one-time manual apply;
      the main config's S3 backend uses `use_lockfile=true` (no DynamoDB), gated by `required_version >= 1.10`.
- [ ] The stateful data volume AND the EIP carry `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` (no silent
      destroy/recreate of Gerrit data on apply/rename/AMI change).
- [ ] `rebar.solutions.navateam.com` resolves (Route 53 A -> EIP in zone Z05558453EZPQLHKC20IQ); SG
      allows 443 + 29418; admin access via SSM Session Manager with the role granting
      `AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore` and no inbound 22.
- [ ] `user_data.sh` pulls the SSM SecureString params at boot to a `chmod 600` file; no plaintext
      secrets appear in user_data.
- [ ] DLM creates daily snapshots of the data volume with bounded retention (e.g. `retain_rule count=7`).
- [ ] `docs/adr/0012-iac-foundations.md` exists and records the three novel architectural decisions with rationale: (1) the S3 state-bucket bootstrap chicken-and-egg pattern, (2) the no-DynamoDB S3-native `use_lockfile` locking choice gated on Terraform >=1.10, and (3) the `/rebar/prod/*` SSM parameter naming contract.
- [ ] A built sdist AND wheel contain NO `infra/` files (deterministic check: `python -m build` then
      grep the artifacts for `infra/`). Homebrew + the MCP registry INHERIT this exclusion because both
      package the same built artifact (the formula builds from the sdist/release; the registry ships the
      wheel) — so the sdist/wheel check is the single source of truth, documented as such.

## Round-7 remediation
- **DLM service role (G6):** AWS Data Lifecycle Manager assumes its OWN IAM role (trust principal `dlm.amazonaws.com`, perms `ec2:CreateSnapshot`/`CreateTags`/`DescribeVolumes`/`DeleteSnapshot`). S1 creates this DLM service role and wires its ARN into the `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` `execution_role_arn` — without it DLM silently creates no snapshots.
- [ ] The DLM lifecycle policy references a created DLM service role (trust `dlm.amazonaws.com`) and is ENABLED (verified it would snapshot the data volume, not silently no-op).

## Plan-review remediation (Round-11, 2026-06-29)
- SSM SecureString scaffolding extended so no downstream lookup fails: ADD `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` (consumed by S7 `monitoring.tf`) and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (consumed by S4a) to the enumerated placeholder params.
- Ownership contracts: S1 CREATES the single EC2 instance role (S2/S4a only add separately-named scoped inline policies, never re-declare it). S1 OWNS the DLM service role + `aws_dlm_lifecycle_policy` (retain count=7); S7 only asserts/monitors it (no second DLM resource).

Added ACs:
- [ ] SSM placeholder set includes `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (in addition to Gerrit secrets, deploy key, HMAC key, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- [ ] The EC2 instance role and the DLM lifecycle policy + DLM service role are created HERE and only here; ADR-0012 records the single-owner contract (S2/S4a attach scoped policies; S7 only monitors DLM)


## Round-12 plan-review remediation (2026-06-29 — recalibrated gate BLOCK)
Two blocking findings fixed; advisories triaged and folded in.

**Blocking (resolved):**
- **B1 — bootstrap state-bucket destruction guard:** the `aws_s3_bucket` in `infra/bootstrap/main.tf` carries `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }` (the state bucket is the third stateful resource alongside the data volume + EIP; without it a `terraform destroy` of the bootstrap stack deletes all TF state, defeating the rebuild-from-Terraform RTO). Destroying it requires an explicit state migration first; recorded in ADR-0012.
- **B2 — idempotent data-volume init:** `user_data.sh` MUST check-before-mkfs — `if ! blkid <device> >/dev/null 2>&1; then mkfs.xfs <device>; fi` — so an instance replacement (e.g. the SSM AMI pointer advancing) re-mounts the existing `/var/gerrit` volume WITHOUT reformatting. `prevent_destroy` guards the Terraform resource; the blkid guard guards the in-guest filesystem (mkfs is a runtime op TF cannot prevent).

**Advisories folded in:**
- **Enumerated SSM param names** (the `/rebar/prod/*` naming contract, recorded in ADR-0012): `/rebar/prod/gerrit-admin-password`, `/rebar/prod/gerrit-ssh-host-ed25519-key`, `/rebar/prod/github-replication-deploy-key`, `/rebar/prod/mcp-hmac-signing-key`, `/rebar/prod/anthropic-api-key`, `/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint`, `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`.
- **Placeholder lifecycle + fail-fast:** params are created with a `CHANGEME` placeholder; the operator MUST populate real values before the S2 apply. `user_data.sh` exits non-zero (cloud-init marks the instance failed) if any fetched value still equals the placeholder sentinel — never silently writes broken config.
- **IAM least privilege:** the instance-role SSM read + `kms:Decrypt` is scoped to `arn:aws:ssm:<region>:<account>:parameter/rebar/prod/*` (no wildcard), referencing `data.aws_caller_identity`.
- **AMI drift:** `aws_instance` carries `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [ami] }`; an AMI upgrade is a deliberate runbook step, not a side effect of a routine apply.
- **Explicit volume sizes:** gp3 root 30 GB, data 50 GB, surfaced as variables.
- **`infra/terraform/variables.tf`** surfaces `snapshot_retention_count` (default 7), `instance_type` (default `t4g.large`), and the volume sizes.

**Added / updated ACs:**
- [ ] `infra/bootstrap/main.tf` state bucket has `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`.
- [ ] `user_data.sh` volume init is idempotent (blkid check before mkfs); a re-run on instance replacement does not reformat the data volume.
- [ ] Instance-role SSM/KMS policy is scoped to `parameter/rebar/prod/*` (no wildcard), verified against the attached policy.
- [ ] `aws_instance` has `lifecycle { ignore_changes = [ami] }`; AMI upgrade is a documented runbook step.
- [ ] The SSM placeholder param set is enumerated by exact name and recorded in ADR-0012; `user_data.sh` fails fast on an unpopulated placeholder sentinel.
- [ ] gp3 volumes have explicit sizes (root 30 GB, data 50 GB) surfaced as Terraform variables in `variables.tf`.
- [ ] ADR-0012 records FOUR decisions: (1) the S3 state-bucket bootstrap chicken-and-egg, (2) no-DynamoDB S3-native `use_lockfile` gated on TF >=1.10, (3) the `/rebar/prod/*` SSM naming contract, (4) the single-owner contract for the EC2 instance role + DLM policy.


## Round-13 plan-review remediation (NVMe device naming on Nitro)
- **B2 refinement — NVMe-aware device resolution:** on t4g.large (Graviton/Nitro) AL2023 presents EBS volumes as NVMe devices (`/dev/nvme1n1`…), NOT the Xen `device_name` (`/dev/xvdf`) Terraform sets. `user_data.sh` therefore resolves the data volume's real device DYNAMICALLY by its EBS volume id rather than hardcoding a name: the volume id is passed into the script via `templatefile()`, and the script matches it against the NVMe controller serial (`for d in /dev/nvme*n1; do nvme id-ctrl -v "$d" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "$VOL_NODASH" && DATA_DEV=$d; done`, or equivalently the AL2023 `/dev/disk/by-id/nvme-Amazon_Elastic_Block_Store_vol...` symlink / `ebsnvme-id`). Only after resolving `DATA_DEV` does the blkid-before-mkfs guard run, so the guard targets the correct device on Nitro. Added AC.
- [ ] `user_data.sh` resolves the data volume's NVMe device path dynamically by EBS volume id (not a hardcoded `/dev/xvdf`); the blkid-before-mkfs guard then runs against the resolved device. Verified the resolved device matches the attached data volume on the live t4g.large.

file_impact.reason: TF>=1.10 + provider pins + S3 backend use_lockfile
EC2 t4g.large, EBS volumes, EIP, ignore_changes ami
instance role + SSM/KMS scoped policy + DLM service role
SSM->.env boot, NVMe-aware idempotent volume init
